title: 'Armed with bamboo sticks': RJD workers step in after Lalu, Rabri lose Z+ security
content: Bihar's political landscape is stirred as Lalu Prasad, Rabri Devi, and Tejashwi Yadav returned security personnel after their cover was downgraded. RJD workers have now taken over guarding Rabri Devi's residence, citing a "bold decision" against perceived political vendetta and insults from the government.

title: 'This doesn't end here': Abhijeet Dipke outlines the next move for CJP
content: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) has issued a seven-day ultimatum to the government, demanding the removal or resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. This follows a large protest at Jantar Mantar, where thousands voiced anger over alleged examination and recruitment irregularities. CJP vows nationwide protests if their demands aren't met.

title: Microsoft CEO to top engineers: Our goal is not to make users 'addicted' to Scout
content: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella has slammed an internal memo that outlined plans to "make people addicted" to Scout, the company's new AI assistant unveiled at Build 2026. In a message to about 50 top engineers, Nadella called the strategy "nonsense" and a "non goal," suggesting the authors "may want to go work elsewhere." The leaked document was authored by Microsoft VP Omar Shahine.

title: 'He is a kid': BCCI to bear parents' expenses as Sooryavanshi begins India journey
content: The BCCI will cover all expenses for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's parents to accompany the 15-year-old on upcoming Ireland and England tours. This move aims to ease the teenage batting prodigy's transition into the senior Indian team environment after a stellar IPL 2026. The board prioritises his comfort and adjustment to international cricket.

title: Banned hookah, ganja, liquor: 156 detained in Pune late-night party raid
content: Pune police raided a party in Tulapur, detaining 156 individuals and seizing liquor worth Rs 9.22 lakh. Three minors were found present, and the event exceeded its permit. Organizers Aiman Sheikh and Yash Chaudhary are identified. This action is part of a broader crackdown on illegal liquor activities following recent fatalities.

title: Meta's highest-paid employee’s 'health message' to Anthropic, OpenAI & Google
content: Meta's top AI executive, Alexandr Wang, revealed the company's strategy to challenge rivals like OpenAI and Google by focusing on health-related AI capabilities. While acknowledging current models aren't top-tier, Wang highlighted Meta's commitment to advancing AI for health applications, aiming to integrate these features into popular platforms like Facebook and Instagram.

title: Indian man shot dead in US after late-night pizza delivery in Philadelphia
content: Tragedy struck North Philadelphia as Indian delivery driver Anshul Kuncha was fatally shot after a pizza delivery to a vacant unit.  Investigators are pursuing a lead from the phone number used for the order. Surveillance footage shows two individuals following Kuncha before the incident. No arrests have been made.

title: 'Mera sapna teeno format khelne ka hai': Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's clear message
content: Young batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi earned a maiden India call-up for Ireland, England tours, and Asian Games after a stellar IPL 2026. The 15-year-old, who became the youngest Indian male player selected, dreams of excelling in all three formats, emphasising his dedication to red-ball cricket despite his explosive T20 success.

title: Brad Pitt and Ines de Ramon enjoy French Open date in Paris
content: In the enchanting backdrop of Paris, Hollywood icon Brad Pitt, 62, was seen relishing the excitement of the French Open alongside his girlfriend, jewellery designer Ines de Ramon, 33. Since becoming a couple in November 2022, their joyful chemistry was evident while they cheered on Mirra Andreeva as she celebrated her maiden Grand Slam victory.

title: Google CEO to entry-level graduates: I don't agree with the troubled AI scenario
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai offers a positive outlook for new graduates amidst AI advancements. He sees AI as a tool that will enhance individual capabilities, making skills more accessible. Pichai also highlights AI's potential to reduce workplace burnout, particularly in demanding professions. While acknowledging disruption, he encourages optimism about AI's positive impact on future careers.

title: 'Echoing Rahul Gandhi': BJP fumes at Revanth Reddy's Hitler inspiration claim
content: Telangana Chief Minister Revanth Reddy has drawn severe criticism from the BJP for claiming Adolf Hitler inspired the name of his task force, HYDRAA. Reddy stated 'Hydra' was Hitler's favorite word and his core team was an assassination squad. The BJP accused the Congress of echoing Rahul Gandhi's language and displaying a 'dangerous Hitler mindset'.

title: 535 Rajasthan farmers die of pesticide exposure in 2 years
content: Rajasthan's fields have become deadly, with 535 farmer deaths from pesticide exposure in two years. Despite Rs 5.1 crore in compensation, stark disparities in payouts and 189 substandard pesticide samples highlight severe safety failures. Authorities are urged to implement stricter regulations and comprehensive safety programs to prevent further tragedies.

title: 'Gill had better credentials than Iyer': Manjrekar explains why Shubman missed out
content: Sanjay Manjrekar believes Shubman Gill possessed stronger credentials for India's next T20I captaincy than Shreyas Iyer. However, Gill's difficulty in securing a consistent playing XI spot reportedly cost him the opportunity. Manjrekar feels Gill's long-term suitability was overlooked, with selectors opting for Iyer due to his guaranteed place in the middle order.

title: Mango Mayday: Dark spots on produce threaten Himsagar export shipments
content: West Bengal's Himsagar mango exports face a setback due to dark spots, a weather-induced disease affecting fruit quality. Continuous rain followed by high temperatures during the bagging stage led to the issue, potentially impacting overseas shipments. Despite this, exporters remain hopeful about fulfilling remaining orders.

title: DU professor murder case: Couple arrested, property dispute suspected behind killing
content: A Delhi University professor, Dr. Debosmita Paul, was found murdered in her east Delhi apartment. Police have arrested a couple known to the victim, with a property dispute suspected as the motive. The 42-year-old English lecturer was discovered by her sister after unanswered calls. Preliminary findings indicate a violent attack with blunt and sharp objects.

title: 'The Last Dance': Neymar signals final WC as Brazil icon eyes one last shot at glory
content: Brazil's football icon Neymar, their all-time leading scorer, has hinted that the 2026 FIFA World Cup will be his final appearance.  Despite a career marked by brilliance and injuries, the 34-year-old forward aims to lead a blend of seasoned and young talent in a quest for Brazil's sixth title, ending a 24-year drought.

title: 'Vishwaguru can't shield its own citizens': Oppn slams Centre over Rs 29 LPG price hike
content: Domestic LPG cylinder prices have risen by Rs 29, with a 14.2-kg cylinder now costing Rs 942 in Delhi. This marks the second increase in three months, drawing sharp criticism from opposition parties who accuse the government of exacerbating inflation and burdening households. The hikes are linked to rising global energy prices amid Middle East conflict.

title: Why female representation in South Indian cinema remains talking point
content: The 'Peddi' controversy highlights concerns over female character portrayal in South Indian cinema, with viewers questioning their depth and purpose. Actress Janhvi Kapoor's role sparked debate, prompting director responses and support from industry peers. This discussion echoes broader criticisms of women's representation in mainstream Indian films, contrasting with Malayalam cinema's nuanced approach.

title: DRS howler! Afghanistan miss Rishabh Pant wicket despite clear edge on UltraEdge
content: Afghanistan missed a crucial DRS opportunity to dismiss Rishabh Pant, who went on to score 81 runs. Replays later confirmed a clear edge on Pant's bat, a decision that proved costly for the visitors. Pant's entertaining knock was eventually ended by captain Hashmatullah Shahidi.

title: Is Israel spying on US? Iran talks come under spotlight as espionage concerns grow
content: US intelligence agencies are reportedly concerned about Israeli espionage targeting American negotiators involved in Iran peace talks. Recent assessments suggest intensified Israeli surveillance on senior US officials, raising counterintelligence threats to a critical level. This comes amid diverging US-Israeli objectives regarding Iran, potentially impacting future military cooperation.

title: 'Shaktimaan' co-star reacts to Mukesh Khanna rejecting Ranveer Singh for the role
content: Shaktimaan co-star Lalit Parimoo suggests Mukesh Khanna's rejection of Ranveer Singh for the iconic role might be a publicity stunt. While praising Ranveer's acting prowess, Parimoo expressed doubts about the film capturing the original show's magic, emphasizing the need for high-tech production to meet modern superhero expectations. Official casting remains unconfirmed. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Lady Gaga's tattoos explained: Stories behind her iconic ink
content: Lady Gaga's body is a living testament to her life's journey, with each tattoo narrating profound personal experiences. From her first treble clef to tributes for David Bowie and John Lennon, these markings chronicle love, survival, and artistic passion.  Her left side, a deliberate choice, holds these stories, a permanent diary etched in ink.

title: Russia downs 339 Ukrainian drones in 13 hours amid Zelenskyy's call to end war
content: In a significant escalation of the conflict, Russia boasts of successfully downing 339 Ukrainian drones over 13 regions in a mere 13 hours, even near Moscow, illustrating the fierce drone warfare at play. Tragically, the impact of Russian offensives continues to be felt in Ukraine, as recovery efforts in Zaporizhzhia uncover lost lives.

title: ‘Stay away’: Elders, imams, functionaries urged Muslim youth to skip CJP protest
content: Ahead of a recent demonstration, a significant section of the Muslim community received widespread warnings urging them to stay away. Social media and mosque announcements advised against participation, citing potential long-term consequences and the risk of being scapegoated if unrest occurred. This caution reflects a growing sentiment following past protests and investigations.

title: MH-53E Sea Dragon: Why US navy's mine-hunting is retiring after 40 years
content: The US Navy's formidable MH-53E Sea Dragon, a vital airborne mine countermeasures helicopter for nearly four decades, is nearing retirement. Its unique ability to clear naval mines from strategic waterways like the Strait of Hormuz is being replaced by newer, autonomous technologies. Despite its immense capability, the aging giant is making way for the future of naval warfare.

title: '5 buildings, 13 plots, mountain of cash': Rs 6k-salaried govt engineer's empire under scanner
content: Odisha Vigilance has unearthed assets worth crores, including over Rs 2.4 crore in cash, from an assistant executive engineer, Baikuntha Nath Behera. Raids across multiple locations revealed five high-rise buildings, 13 plots, gold ornaments, and significant bank deposits. The investigation into disproportionate assets is ongoing.

title: Carlsen left in awe of Praggnanandhaa's Norway Chess heroics: 'That's pretty insane'
content: Indian Grandmaster R Praggnanandhaa achieved a historic victory at Norway Chess 2026, becoming the first Indian to win the prestigious title. He secured the championship with a stunning comeback in the final round, defeating Vincent Keymer and benefiting from a draw between his rivals. World No. 1 Magnus Carlsen lauded Praggnanandhaa's "clutch" performance under pressure.

title: Saved by the barrel: Why crude hasn't hit the $200 mark
content: Despite fears of $200 oil following Strait of Hormuz disruptions, prices remain below $100. This resilience is attributed to increased US exports, weaker Chinese demand, and alternative supply routes. While some shipping through the strait continues, overall transits are down. Emergency measures and subdued demand have averted the worst-case scenario, but market stability hinges on temporary solutions.

title: Watch: Eggs hurled at TMC MLA Madan Mitra's vehicle amid protest in Kamarhati
content: Senior TMC MLA Madan Mitra faced protests in Ariadaha, with residents allegedly hurling eggs at his vehicle amid demands for repayment of "cut money." This incident follows a pattern of public anger targeting TMC leaders post-election defeat, fueled by corruption allegations. Similar demonstrations have occurred across the state, reflecting widespread discontent.

title: 'What else he need to do?' Harbhajan fumes over Rajat Patidar's India snub
content: Former India spinner Harbhajan Singh questioned selectors after Rajat Patidar's omission from the T20I squad. Patidar had a stellar IPL 2026 season, scoring 501 runs at a strike rate of nearly 200, and previously led RCB to back-to-back titles. Harbhajan believes Patidar is the best middle-order batter in India.

title: 'Trailer of what is coming next': CJP says 'cockroaches showed their strength' in first protest
content: Hundreds gathered at Jantar Mantar demanding Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over examination and recruitment irregularities. The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) called the protest a 'trailer' for a larger movement, vowing fresh demonstrations if Pradhan isn't sacked within seven days. Participants wore cockroach masks, highlighting concerns over NEET, CUET, and SSC processes.

title: Poonam Dhillon praises Ranveer Singh for offering compensation in 'Don 3' row: 'Kudos to him'
content: CINTAA president Poonam Dhillon defended Ranveer Singh amid the 'Don 3' dispute, praising his professionalism and offering compensation after his exit. Dhillon emphasized actors' right to creative input, stating their intelligence and projection warrant a say in scripts for a project's betterment. The Producers Guild is now mediating the issue. Read on to know more in detail.

title: ViKing of Norway: Pragg wins crown that eluded even Vishy
content: R Praggnanandhaa has achieved a historic victory at Norway Chess, a feat even Vishy Anand couldn't accomplish. The young Indian Grandmaster secured the title by defeating Vincent Keymer in the final round, overcoming fatigue from prior tournaments. His remarkable comeback, including four consecutive classical wins, has placed him among chess elite, proving his exceptional talent.

title: Monaco GP: Antonelli stuns Ferrari duo to snatch pole, Verstappen joins front row
content: Nineteen-year-old Kimi Antonelli secured a stunning pole position at the Monaco Grand Prix, defying expectations and Mercedes' first pole of the decade.  Despite Ferrari's strong practice pace, Antonelli's decisive final lap edged out Max Verstappen, setting the stage for a thrilling strategic battle.

title: Hours after replacing Surya as India T20I captain, Shreyas leads side to win over SKY
content: Shreyas Iyer, newly appointed India's T20I captain, marked his first day by leading SoBo Mumbai Falcons to victory over Suryakumar Yadav's Triumph Knights MNE in the T20 Mumbai League. Iyer's commanding 61 off 36 balls steered his team to a five-wicket win, overshadowing Suryakumar's 48. Despite the captaincy change, Suryakumar expressed pride and support for Iyer's appointment.

title: LPG gets Rs 29 costlier: Check latest cylinder rates in Delhi, Mumbai and other cities
content: Domestic cooking gas prices have increased by Rs 29 per cylinder, reaching Rs 942 in Delhi, marking the second revision in three months due to soaring global energy costs. This rise follows a previous Rs 60 hike and is part of a broader trend of fuel price increases, with petrol and diesel also seeing significant jumps.

title: Iran peace deal at risk? US considers redirecting Iranian assets to Gulf states
content: The US is exploring redirecting frozen Iranian assets to Gulf states for rebuilding after missile and drone strikes. This move comes as indirect negotiations between the US and Iran remain deadlocked, with Tehran demanding the release of $24 billion in frozen funds. Military tensions continue to escalate across the region despite ongoing diplomatic efforts.

title: Telangana schools to reopen on June 15, not 12, clarifies School Education Department
content: However, the second Saturday next month (July 11) will be treated as a regular working day for all the schools

title: Survey in Rajasthan to scrutinise unauthorised constructions, funding in border areas
content: The exercise covers all constructions within 15 kilometres of the international border and follows directions issued by Union Home Minister Amit Shah during a high-level security review meeting in Bikaner last month

title: Despite last-minute preparations, visitor numbers during summer festival season increased 28% in 2026
content: With the election results being declared in May and a new government taking charge, preparations for the annual shows — the Rose Show, Flower Show, Plantation Crops Show and Fruit Show, were delayed, officials said

title: Trinamool's minority cell secretary Ajmal Siddiqui resigns, blames Abhishek Banerjee for party ‘collapse’
content: "The party is collapsing today solely because of one man: Abhishek Banerjee. His dictatorial attitude and the oppression we faced — false cases filed against us 12 or 13 years ago, demands for money — were unbearable," Ajmal Siddiqui said

title: T.N. government’s decision to shelve smart meters project a grave mistake: BJP
content: Smart meters would create a robust infrastructure fully supported by loans and grants from Central government agencies, along with private capital, Narayanan Thirupathy said

title: Security beefed up at CJP founder Dipke's home in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar
content: Earlier, 11 security personnel were deployed; now the number has reached 15; the deployed staff is from the local police station as well as the city police headquarters, said an official from MIDC Waluj police station

title: LPG domestic cylinder now costs ₹957.50 in Chennai, consumers express shock
content: In a span of three months it has gone up by ₹89/14.2 kg cylinder. Customers who have already booked their refills but have not received the same, will have to pay the revised rate

title: Why aren’t BJP leaders taking to streets with cylinders in protest now: Congress’ dig on LPG price hike
content: Mallikarjun Kharge said Prime Minister Narendra Modi had made "grand claims" in Parliament about diversifying fuel sources across 41 countries in response to the West Asia conflict and asked what became of that

title: Tamil Nadu Governor leads plantation drive, lays foundation stone for PLAZA in Auroville
content: The government formally announced that Auroville plans to plant more than 10,000 trees in the near future to further enrich its dense, sustainable urban forest

title: Chief Minister travels by Metro to avoid Zero-Traffic arrangements in Bengaluru
content: Shivakumar is visiting Kanakapura, his home turf, first time after becoming the Karnataka Chief Minister to meet and extend his gratitude to the people of his constituency

title: Congress aims for organisation overhaul, agitations from June 18 to corner BJP Govt in Goa
content: The reconstitution of the Goa Pradesh Congress Committee executive and other organisational bodies will soon be announced as part of a comprehensive restructuring exercise

title: Collector launches Yogandhra 2026 program
content: The ‘Yogandhra-2026’ program would be conducted over a period of 14 days, from June 7 leading up to International Yoga Day on June 21.

title: IDFC First Bank-AU Finance Bank fraud case: CBI conducts searches at six locations
content: The searches were carried out on Friday (June 5, 2026) as part of an ongoing probe into the alleged misappropriation of funds parked with IDFC First Bank and AU Finance Bank, an official statement said

title: Our agitation will continue until Union Minister Pradhan resigns: Cockroach Janta Party founder Dipke
content: Mr. Dipke also said their protest at Jantar Mantar in New Delhi on Saturday (June 6) was largely successful, with around 7,000 people participating in it, and added that the movement would now expand nationwide

title: LPG Price Hike: Government says rates in India among world's lowest despite 46% jump in global benchmark
content: The government said the cost of supplying a domestic LPG cylinder has risen to more than ₹1,600 following a surge in international prices that followed the outbreak of war in West Asia at the end February

title: CJP’s agitation to continue until Union minister Pradhan resigns: Abhijeet Dipke
content: The CJP, a youth-led online movement that has been demanding accountability for alleged irregularities in examinations and recruitment tests, including NEET, CBSE, CUET, and SSC examinations

title: Ladakh L-G to lead Indian delegation to bring Buddha relics back from Mongolia
content: The visit comes in the wake of the successful exposition of the sacred Piprahwa relics of Lord Buddha in Ladakh from May 1 to 14 this year

title: Railways to invest ₹1 lakh crore in upgrading infrastructure in West Bengal
content: The Minister also said that the proposed bullet train services between Delhi–Varanasi and Varanasi–Siliguri were aimed at sharply reducing travel time

title: Telangana DCA issues notices to 41 online pharmacy-linked medical shops after statewide inspections
content: The inspections covered 166 medical shops, including 159 establishments associated with online medicine delivery services and 7 shops linked to online business-to-business (B2B) platforms such as IndiaMART

title: Indonesian FM Sugiono arrives in New Delhi to co-chair 8th Joint Commission Meeting with EAM Jaishankar
content: Accompanied by Indonesia’s lower house Representative Marlyn Maisarah, Sugionothe Foreign Minister is leading a high-level delegation to the national capital

title: Domestic LPG price hiked by ₹29 per 14.2-kg cylinder
content: The increase follows a ₹60-per-cylinder hike on March 7 after the conflict in West Asia disrupted global energy supplies and drove up international fuel prices.

title: K. Annamalai | New chapter
content: The former BJP Tamil Nadu president has quit the party and launched a new ‘movement’, promising to bring ‘constructive’ and ‘clean’ politics

title: Goa Forest Official submits police complaint against Minister over soil transportation from forest
content: The complaint alleged trees were illegally uprooted during the activity, which amounted to violation of forest laws and government policies aimed at increasing green cover

title: Security forces urge Manipur villagers to sensitise youths to not get carried away by violence
content: The development came in the wake of the killing of three civilians in a gun attack at Loibol Khullen village in Kangpokpi district on Friday (June 5, 2026)

title: Salim Kumar: a mimicry artiste par excellence before taking over the film arena
content: The art brought Salim Kumar fame and made him a sought-after performer in the famed Kalabhavan troupe and in foreign shows; it paved the way for his entry into the film industry

title: Salim Kumar: the ultimate meme king
content: For three years after winning the National Award in 2011, the genial actor had stayed away from silver screen due to poor health. Yet he remained alive in public memory, thanks largely to memes that liberally drew on his image and witty dialogues, giving them a sharp satirical edge

title: Fire at residential building in Navi Mumbai; four injured
content: Ten persons were rescued from the premises. A minor and three other persons suffered burn injuries and breathing difficulties, the official said

title: Versatile actor Salim Kumar passes away aged 57
content: He had been rushed to a private hospital with fever and shortness of breath in the early hours of June 6, and placed on ventilator support

title: Tributes paid to Army officer killed during anti-terror operations in J&K's Rajouri
content: The officer died after slipping in a mountainous area and falling into a deep gorge during an anti-terror operation in Rajouri district on Saturday (June 6, 2026) evening

title: From ignominy, Salim Kumar took firm strides to national acclaim
content: In 1997, he was dropped from a film for his ‘lack of acting prowess.’ Thirteen years later, the National Film Award came his way through Adaminte Makan Abu. Salim Kumar, who has acted in 250-odd films, was a lively presence in the Malayalam film industry for three decades, switching effortlessly from comedy to character roles.

title: G.O. granting promotion to seven IAS officers to Chief Secretary grade challenged before Madras High Court
content: Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan wonder how a public interest litigation petition can be filed in a government service related matter

title: Three villagers killed, houses torched in Manipur; CM condemns attack
content: Chief Minister Yumnam Khemchand Singh condemns the killings; Kuki Inpi Manipur blames NSCN and a Zeliangrong United Front faction for the attack on Loibol Khullen village

title: Kukis take out ‘coffin rally’ in Manipur, CRPF chief reviews security
content: Mourners demand justice for three victims of the attack at Loibol Khullen village in Kangpokpi district on June 5

title: Vedant Shrivastava, Nisarga Adhikary and Sarthak Sidhant | The Gen Z trio that took on the CBSE
content: The three students who were viciously trolled for raising their voice on public platforms against the CBSE’s On-Screen Marking system stand vindicate dafter the Board admitted to flaws in the system

title: Why did Supreme Court back curbs on online gaming?
content: What were the two sets of appeals concerning the regulation and taxation of online gaming platforms in front of the Court? What were the main objections raised by gaming companies against the GST levy? What are the likely consequences for India’s online gaming industry?

title: BJP names Rajya Sabha candidates for five States; two Union Ministers left out
content: Ravneet Singh Bittu and George Kurian not renominated as party fields mix of organisational leaders and younger faces

title: Delhi Police allowed CJP’s protest to ‘pacify’ burgeoning youth anger; Dipke secured approval in minutes
content: After analysing the chatter on social media and getting a sense of the situation on the ground, it was felt that refusing permission may have led to some protesters storming the venue or the police station; the confrontation would have further fuelled the anger among the youth, government sources say

title: Digvijaya Singh urges PM Modi to put three-language policy implementation on hold
content: “I am forwarding herewith a representation received from a group of concerned parents of CBSE Class IX students, opposing the mandatory implementation of the three-language policy in the current mid-session,” Mr. Singh said

title: TDP announces three candidates for Rajya Sabha elections
content: The TDP stated that the selections were made with a focus on social engineering and dedication and loyalty to the party and to encourage young leadership

title: Late-night Pune party with over 150 attendees raided; ganja, illegal smoking items seized
content: A total of 156 persons, including 107 men and 49 women, were found at the venue. Three of them were below 21 years of age

title: AI will reshape jobs, but India’s bigger challenge is preparing workers, boardrooms and classrooms
content: ‘You need humans to frame questions and inputs, AI does the work, and then you need humans again at the end to verify the outcome’

title: The Hindu Huddle concludes with evocative musical performance by former diplomat Nirupama Rao
content: Ms. Rao, who is also the founder of South Asian Symphony Foundation (SASF), took to the stage in accompaniment with Sri Lankan pianist Soundarie David Rodrigo as the former envoy blended her experience of diplomacy with songs to end the event on a musical high

title: The Hindu Huddle | Manoj Bajpayee wants an image makeover
content: You cannot recreate human experience; the real-time performance is always authentic when compared to AI, says the actor in a light-hearted chat with Rohit Khilnani

title: Nagpur NEET aspirant's note not attached to case papers; show-cause notice issued to Investigation Officer
content: “The note left behind by the deseased was mentioned in the station diary entry, but was not attached to the case papers,” said the Deputy Commissioner of Police Nityanand Jha

title: Memories, Medium, or Message? Art as mirror to a world in transition
content: The role of art institutions, how art must respond to a shifting socio-political landscape and its power to shape collective opinion were discussed

title: Why is there a rebellion in the Trinamool Congress? | Explained
content: What event triggered the rebellion within the Trinamool Congress legislature party? Why were MLAs Ritabrata Banerjee and Sandipan Saha expelled from the party? What challenges do Mamata Banerjee and the party face?

title: Some forces trying to push country’s youths towards ‘negative politics’: BJP chief Nitin Nabin
content: “Some forces are trying to make the country's youths anti-establishment,” Mr. Nabin said during an interaction with intellectuals in Ranchi.

title: Encounter actions in U.P. undermining criminal justice system, making society barbaric, says former U.P. top police officials
content: Sulkhan Singh, a former Uttar Pradesh Director General of Police (DGP) said through encounters the State is normalizing violence, ultimately creating a climate of vigilantism

title: NAC-AP wins dual honours at IINA Awards 2026
content: The awards, presented by Dinesh Sharma, Member of Parliament and former Deputy Chief Minister of Uttar Pradesh, recognises contributions to the nation’s infrastructure and skill development sectors

title: Pre-monsoon downpour drenches parts of Hyderabad
content: The showers led to waterlogging at multiple locations and slowed vehicular movement; Saturday’s downpour marked the first significant spell of rain ahead of the monsoon season

title: India, Nepal can decisively shift trajectory of bilateral ties to achieve full potential: Jaishankar
content: Nepal wants to pursue “result-driven diplomacy” with India, its “most important partner”, says Nepal FM; “strong complementarities” between the current governments in Delhi, Kathmandu, says EAM

title: CJP warns of nationwide agitation if Education Minister Pradhan not removed in seven days
content: According to CJP spokespersons Ashutosh Ranka, removing the Education Minister was necessary if meaningful reforms were to be undertaken.

title: AIADMK to contest bypolls for 4 Assembly constituencies, says Edappadi K. Palaniswami
content: Speaking at a public meeting to thank the voters of his constituency of Edappadi, he alleges that the TVK is the same as the DMK and the people will soon understand about the TVK government

title: Shut but open | T.N.’s closure of liquor shops
content: Every time a new party comes to power, closing liquor shops becomes one of its standard promises. But shops are eventually opened in other locations. Is the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam  government’s decision to shut 717 liquor shops near educational institutions and places of worship going to be any different, people wonder

title: News in Frames | Trickle of freshwater just off the salty sea
content: Pits dug on the beach serve as freshwater source for fishing villages near Mimisal in Pudukottai, Tamil Nadu; residents say they are a surviving link to the community’s history, tradition, and resilience; they raise quality concerns over littering

title: Hopes and fears compete as Delhi students join CJP protest for education reforms
content: Student organisations support demand for Education Minister’s resignation but are wary of endorsing the CJP itself, worried about its links to the AAP and the wider goals of the movement

title: Human activities claimed 1,653 elephant lives in India since 2009
content: T C Sreemol, Principal Correspondent at The Times of India, has been with the publication for 14 years. She extensively covers the environment and forests, animal welfare, civic issues in urban Kochi, and migrant workers in Kerala. She also specialises in data-driven stories. Read More

title: Planning to rent out your house? Here are 7 things every landlord should know
content: The TOI Real Estate Desk is a focused team of seasoned journalists and market watchers dedicated to decoding the ever-evolving property landscape for The Times of India readers. With a sharp eye on trends, policy shifts, and market movements, the team brings clarity to one of the most significant investment decisions in people’s lives. From expert insights on buying, selling, and investing to deep dives into infrastructure developments, home design, and sustainable living, the news here offers a comprehensive view of the real estate ecosystem. Whether you're a first-time homebuyer, a seasoned investor, or simply exploring the market, the TOI Real Estate Desk is your trusted guide to making informed property decisions. Read More

title: The bird that woke us up has fallen silent. Can we bring back the Sparrow's chirp?
content: the song of the birds is no longer the soundtrack of dawn . the sparrow population has decreased by nearly 64% in Europe since the 1970s . birdlife international reports that the population has dropped by nearly 6% in Europe .

title: Court convicts Bihar BJP MLA in New Year bash firing case
content: the case relates to celebratory firing during a party on Dec 31, 2018, at a farmhouse in Fatehpur Beri in Delhi . a woman, archana Gupta, was hit by a bullet in the head and died in the hospital on Jan 3, 2019 .

title: 1965-Built Monastery Destroyed In Sikkim Fire, Religious Artefacts Damaged
content: No casualties or injuries were reported in the incident. However, the fire resulted in property losses and the complete destruction of irreplaceable religious artefacts

title: US Iran War Live Updates: US Shoots Down 2 Iranian Drones, Pak Minister Visits Tehran
content: US Iran War Live Updates:United States military officials said Iran launched seven ballistic missiles toward neighboring Gulf nations of Kuwait and Bahrain.

title: Israel Spying On Trump's Iran Negotiators? US Intelligence Raises Concerns
content: Intelligence reports have suggested that Israeli spy agencies may have intensified surveillance activities aimed at understanding the US negotiating position in talks with Iran.

title: Bengal Couple Travelled 1,400 km To Kill Delhi Professor. How Cops Found Them
content: Debosmita Paul, an assistant professor at Shivaji College, was murdered in her flat at Satyam Apartments in Vasudhara Enclave on Wednesday.

title: Senior Royals Gather As King Charles' Nephew Marries Nurse Harriet Sperling

title: Border standoff ends, BSF sends stranded ‘illegals’ to Bangladesh
